grade.details: The agent successfully navigated to the Wikipedia World population article, extracted the correct table data using JavaScript evaluation, and reported all 8 billion milestones (1804, 1927, 1960, 1974, 1987, 1999, 2011, 2022) matching the known facts exactly.

agent_output:
Here are the years when world population reached each billion milestone, per the Wikipedia article's table (Worldometers estimates):

| Population | Year Reached | Years Since Previous Billion |
|------------|-------------|------------------------------|
| 1 billion  | 1804        | —                            |
| 2 billion  | 1927        | 123 years                    |
| 3 billion  | 1960        | 33 years                     |
| 4 billion  | 1974        | 14 years                     |
| 5 billion  | 1987        | 13 years                     |
| 6 billion  | 1999        | 12 years                     |
| 7 billion  | 2011        | 12 years                     |
| 8 billion  | 2022        | 11 years                     |

The trend shows a dramatic acceleration: it took all of recorded history until 1804 to reach 1 billion people, then just 123 years to double to 2 billion, and the pace kept accelerating — each subsequent billion has been added in roughly 11–14 years.

---

agent_output:
Here's the report:

## https://httpbin.org/status/404

**Error encountered:** The page loaded a Chrome error page (`chrome-error://chromewebdata/`) with:
> "This httpbin.org page can't be found — No webpage was found for the web address: https://httpbin.org/status/404 — **HTTP ERROR 404**"

The server returned a 404 Not Found response, which Chrome treated as an unrenderable page and displayed its built-in error screen.

---
